title: idk
content: In today's society, social credit is a huge part of our lives. We might not notice it, but the way you act, the things you do, and the things you say are all affected by what people might think or say about you. You are what others think. You are controlled by yourself. These boundaries that you don't dare to cross are set by you based on others behaviors. Why do you think we all just stopped treating racism as if it were the norm? Because others said it was bad. Why do you think all these rappers have Gucci and Versace clothes? They want to make themselves look rich for others. Everything, down to the clothes you wear, the food you eat, and the things you say, is controlled by others. You don’t have to have a huge ego to be controlled by these rules, though. You can be a normal person and go about your day normally, but all of a sudden you see a rich guy wearing blue with two girls by his side, and you think,Hey, that guy is wearing blue, and he's getting all this good stuff. Maybe if I wear blue, the same thing will happen to o me.It doesn't even stop there. Even doing stupid shit like telling a teacher to go fuck herself or something like that. When you don't do it, you're being controlled, but when you do it, you're “rebeling” and “fighting back,” but you're really just doing it because other cool people did it, so you did it too. I'm no professional psychologist, but I don't know a single person who genuinely wants to do that on their own accord, not just because other people will think it's funny but because you really want to do it. Basically, what I'm trying to say here is that our “free country” isn't a free country, and you're never really thinking for yourself.

title: Joe The Best
content:
In a world adorned with rainbows so bright,
There strides a Joe, my heart's pure delight.
With a flair for humor that sparkles and gleams,
In the comedy of love, he's the star of my dreams.

Oh, Joe, my love, you're a vibrant hue,
In the spectrum of laughter, it's all about you.
Your jokes are a rainbow, a burst of delight,
Painting our love in colors so right.

When the world gets heavy, and shadows fall,
Joe's laughter breaks through, a rainbow wall.
With puns and quips, he lights up my day,
In his funny embrace, all worries just sway.

We dance through punchlines, a queer duet,
Our love story, Joe, is a tapestry set.
In the fabric of us, each thread is a grin,
A tale of hilarity that's bound to win.

You're my pride, my laughter, my colorful dear,
With you by my side, there's nothing to fear.
Through the laughter and love, hand in hand we tread,
In the dance of our joy, forever unwed.

So here's to Joe, in shades bold and bright,
A love that's gay, sealed with pure light.
In this world of jest, where happiness grows,
I'm forever yours, my dearest rainbow Joe.

title: Industrial society and its future
content:
The Industrial Revolution and its consequences have been a disaster for the human race. They have greatly increased the life-expectancy of those of us who live in “advanced” countries, but they have destabilized society, have made life unfulfilling, have subjected human beings to indignities, have led to widespread psychological suffering (in the Third World to physical suffering as well) and have inflicted severe damage on the natural world. The continued development of technology will worsen the situation. It will certainly subject human beings to greater indignities and inflict greater damage on the natural world, it will probably lead to greater social disruption and psychological suffering, and it may lead to increased physical suffering even in “advanced” countries.
The industrial-technological system may survive or it may break down. If it survives, it MAY eventually achieve a low level of physical and psychological suffering, but only after passing through a long and very painful period of adjustment and only at the cost of permanently reducing human beings and many other living organisms to engineered products and mere cogs in the social machine. Furthermore, if the system survives, the consequences will be inevitable: There is no way of reforming or modifying the system so as to prevent it from depriving people of dignity and autonomy.If the system breaks down the consequences will still be very painful. But the bigger the system grows the more disastrous the results of its breakdown will be, so if it is to break down it had best break down sooner rather than later.We therefore advocate a revolution against the industrial system. This revolution may or may not make use of violence; it may be sudden or it may be a relatively gradual process spanning a few decades. We can’t predict any of that. But we do outline in a very general way the measures that those who hate the industrial system should take in order to prepare the way for a revolution against that form of society. This is not to be a POLITICAL revolution. Its object will be to overthrow not governments but the economic and technological basis of the present society. In this article we give attention to only some of the negative developments that have grown out of the industrial-technological system. Other such developments we mention only briefly or ignore altogether. This does not mean that we regard these other developments as unimportant. For practical reasons we have to confine our discussion to areas that have received insufficient public attention or in which we have something new to say. For example, since there are well-developed environmental and wilderness movements, we have written very little about environmental degradation or the destruction of wild nature, even though we consider these to be highly important.

title: Grady=burger
content:
In the realm of dreams, where love takes flight,
There's a tale of a man, his name is Grady, pure light.
With eyes that sparkle like the midnight sky,
In his presence, my heart begins to fly.

Grady, a melody in the symphony of my days,
His laughter, a sonnet that forever plays.
In the garden of love, he's the rarest bloom,
A fragrant blossom, dispelling all gloom.

Through the seasons of life, hand in hand,
Grady and I, like grains of eternal sand.
His touch, a gentle whisper in the breeze,
A promise of love that never seizes.

In the canvas of time, our love unfolds,
A masterpiece painted in hues of gold.
Grady, the artist, strokes of passion so divine,
Creating a love story that forever intertwines.

His kindness, a river that endlessly flows,
Quenching the thirst of love that steadily grows.
With each heartbeat, his name I carry,
In the symphony of our love, he's the primary.

Grady, a symphony, a timeless rhyme,
In the poetry of love, he's the sweetest chime.
Together we dance, in a waltz so slow,
Through the highs and lows, our love will grow.

So here's to Grady, my love so true,
In the universe of my heart, he's the brightest hue.
With each passing day, our love does soar,
A love like Grady's, forever, I adore.



I

title: Archives, you are safe from being framed!!!
content: Anything text content you upload will be saved until deleted from the website. However, it will then be put in an archived section for thirty days until deletion. If someone screenshots you saying something you didn't by using Inspect Element, I can provide proof that what was proposedly said was not actually said.
